singapore.summary: Avoid swatting at the insects, as this may provoke them and trigger a more aggressive response.

singapore.title: Man made to retire without notice or payment wins S$26,350 in claims against employer
singapore.summary: After asking about re-employment, the man was offered a six-month role at lower pay and given a non-negotiable one-week deadline to decide.

singapore.summary: Weeks after a bumper harvest flooded the market with cheap durians, sellers say the oversupply has begun to ease as Johor's durian season winds down.

singapore.summary: The syndicate began with one SMS modem and expanded with multiple members, with the group shifting into an empty office space where it stored its 27 SMS modems and 24,000 SIM cards.

us.title: The star-studded World Cup semifinals kick off with France vs. Spain
us.summary: The expanded World Cup allowed FIFA to introduce a new seeding system that placed the world's top teams in opposite corners of the knockout bracket. Now, the semifinals are both heavyweight matchups.

us.title: UAE says Iran hit two of its tankers, killing one crew member
us.summary: The defense ministry of the United Arab Emirates (UAE) said Monday Iranian forces targeted a pair of its tankers in the Strait of Hormuz, killing one crew member. The UAE Ministry of Defence wrote on social platform X Iranian cruise missiles hit the country’s Mombasa and Al Bahiyah tankers, which were transiting the southern lane…

us.title: Esper: Trump reimposing naval blockade on Iran ‘the right move’
us.summary: Former Defense Secretary Mark Esper said Monday that President Trump made the “right move” in resuming the U.S. naval blockade of Iranian ports. “I agree with the president, I think it’s the right move to reinstate the blockade,” Esper, who ran the Pentagon during Trump’s first term, said on CNN’s “Erin Burnett OutFront.” Earlier Monday,…

So I really don’t know,” Johnson told host Eric Bolling on Real America’s Voice’s “Bolling!”…

In a slew of posts on Truth Social, Trump threw his support behind John Cowan in Georgia’s 11th Congressional District, Anthony DiLorenzo in…

us.title: Lettuce or salad greens may be culprit in Michigan cyclosporiasis outbreak, health officials say
us.summary: A statewide outbreak of a parasitic illness that can cause “explosive” diarrhea may be linked to lettuce or salad greens, health officials say.

us.title: Trump's HHS abandons threat to withhold Medicare and Medicaid funding over trans care

asia.title: How people’s genes are revealing why some languages are so unique
asia.summary: A new study measured how different neighbouring languages were from one another. The authors say that linguistic hotspots may offer glimpses into how human languages became more varied before large migrations and population movements reshaped much of the world

asia.title: Philippine rice output risks 30% collapse as ‘super’ El Nino strengthens
asia.summary: In the fields of central Luzon, farmers who planted their rice in June are watching the sky with worry. Out over the Pacific, a familiar spectre is gathering strength – and with it, the threat of empty granaries and hungry households. The Philippines has a plan for this year’s “super” El Nino, on paper at least. But to the Filipinos out actually working the paddies, that plan is barely perceptible. “We don’t see anything visible, it’s all talk,” said Raul Montemayor, national manager of the...

asia.title: India-Australia ‘administrative arrangements’ for uranium supplies | Explained
asia.summary: The recent agreement paves the way for Australian uranium exports to India, allowing private sector collaboration under stringent IAEA oversight

asia.title: U.S. immigration agents involved in another fatal shooting

asia.title: Viral photos of heavy-duty drones rescuing China’s flood victims spark debate
asia.summary: Photos and discussions about China’s recent use of heavy-duty drones to “airlift” villagers affected by flooding in Guangxi have gone viral but almost two decades ago, the disaster-prone country once relied on risky, low-tech means to fight natural calamities. China’s rapid tech advancement, represented by the development and deployment of drones, has helped it better handle emergencies, such as how first responders have been rescuing residents from widespread rainstorms and floods since...

briefing: Global tensions escalated overnight as the UAE reported that Iranian forces targeted two of its tankers in the Strait of Hormuz, killing one crew member, while former US Defense Secretary Mark Esper expressed support for President Trump's decision to reimpose a naval blockade on Iranian ports. In Singapore, a local man was awarded S$26,350 in claims against his employer after being made to retire without notice or payment, highlighting the importance of fair employment practices. Meanwhile, the Singapore durian market is experiencing a price surge as the oversupply from Johor's durian season begins to ease, with sellers noting that prices have started to rise again.

digests.singapore: Singapore has seen a range of developments, including a warning about the dangers of hornet attacks and the importance of not provoking them, as well as a significant legal victory for a man who was made to retire without notice or payment, winning $26,350 in claims against his employer. The local economy grew 5.7% in Q2, driven by a 12.2% expansion in the manufacturing sector, although this represents a slight easing from the previous quarter's 6.3% growth. Additionally, a syndicate involved in scams has been dismantled, with a financier jailed, and various other events are taking place, including concerts and diplomatic celebrations.
digests.us: The US is involved in various international incidents, including a naval blockade of Iran, which former Defense Secretary Mark Esper supports, and a reported attack by Iran on UAE tankers in the Strait of Hormuz. Domestically, President Trump is endorsing House GOP candidates ahead of the midterms, while the Department of Health and Human Services has abandoned a threat to withhold Medicare and Medicaid funding over trans care. Additionally, a federal immigration officer fatally shot a man in Maine, citing fear for public safety, and health officials are investigating a parasitic illness outbreak in Michigan potentially linked to lettuce or salad greens.
digests.asia: The Asia-Pacific region is experiencing a range of challenges, including health risks from Ebola in Congo, which has prompted the US to restrict travel, and environmental disasters such as floods in China and a potential 30% collapse in Philippine rice output due to a "super" El Nino. Geopolitical tensions are also escalating, with the US striking Iran for the third night and India and Australia finalizing administrative arrangements for uranium supplies. Meanwhile, technological advancements are being leveraged to respond to emergencies, such as China's use of heavy-duty drones to rescue flood victims, while other incidents like a fatal music bar fire in Bangkok highlight ongoing safety concerns.
digests.others: Tensions between the US and Iran continue to escalate, with the US launching fresh strikes and threatening to attack a nuclear facility, while the UAE condemns Iran's attack on tankers. Meanwhile, human rights and freedom of expression are under scrutiny in India, where a film about a human rights activist has been blocked, and in the US, where links between government officials and disgraced financier Jeffrey Epstein are being uncovered. Global trade talks are also stalled, with a US trade expert warning that trilateral talks are needed to secure a cross-border trade deal between the US, Mexico, and Canada.
digests.tech: The technology and innovation headlines focus on advancements in database management, sustainable energy, and space exploration, with notable developments including the introduction of YouTrackDB, a method to recover up to 90% of lithium from used EV batteries, and SpaceX's upcoming 13th test flight of Starship. Additionally, there are discussions on the fundamentals of wireless communication and the potential impact of automation on future work. The headlines also touch on the intersection of technology and society, including the use of satellites and concerns about the role of humans in a rapidly changing technological landscape.
digests.economist: The Economist's analysis highlights disparities in social and economic spheres, including the scarcity of female breadwinners in America and inadequate infrastructure development in India, which has not kept pace with prosperity. Meanwhile, global power dynamics are shifting, with potential threats to America's financial supremacy and Red Bull's marketing dominance, as well as ongoing geopolitical tensions in Ukraine and Myanmar. Additionally, the publication touches on social and health issues, such as dementia incidence rates and the debate over the age of criminal responsibility.
